focus: correctness: verify renamed markdown runtime files, preserved /create:* command-family references, and behavior-inconsistent old identity references

findingDetails.title: Codex agent registry still points at the removed create agent
findingDetails.evidence: Codex registry declares [agents.create] and config_file = "agents/create.toml" while the renamed Codex mirror exists as .codex/agents/markdown.toml with name = "markdown"; old create.toml was absent by scoped glob.
findingDetails.recommendation: Rename the Codex registry entry to markdown and point config_file at agents/markdown.toml while preserving /create:* command strings.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Spec requires markdown identity references except preserved create commands; implementation summary claims Codex mirror moved to markdown.toml; scoped old-identity grep found .codex/config.toml as the remaining Codex hit.

noveltyJustification: One new P1 was found in a Codex runtime registry surface not covered by the packet's recorded old-reference verification command; no prior findings existed.
edgeCases: Dispatch omitted convergence before initial binding; config later established 0.1.
.codex/config.toml was not in the initial files-under-review table but was directly implicated by the Codex mirror rename and old-file absence check.
ruledOut: /create:* command names are intentionally preserved and not stale identity references.
No P0 correctness issue was identified in this pass.
integrationEvidence: runtime markdown files expose markdown identity

focus: traceability/resource_map_coverage: cross-check spec, implementation-summary, checklist, tasks, and resource-map claims against renamed runtime files, command surfaces, and active Codex registry mismatch

findingDetails.title: Codex agent registry still points at the removed create agent
findingDetails.evidence: Carry-forward/refinement: .codex/config.toml still declares [agents.create] and config_file = "agents/create.toml" at lines 62-64 while implementation-summary.md:65 claims the Codex runtime mirror moved to .codex/agents/markdown.toml. resource-map.md:64-67 verification commands cover .codex/agents and old file absence but omit .codex/config.toml, so resource-map verification can pass without proving Codex registry routing follows the rename.
findingDetails.recommendation: Rename the Codex registry entry to markdown and point config_file at agents/markdown.toml, or document an intentional legacy alias and add explicit resource-map/checklist evidence for it.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Spec Files to Change and resource-map write paths include Codex agent mirrors, but not the Codex registry consumer; direct read confirms the registry still points to agents/create.toml.

noveltyJustification: No new finding ID was added. Iteration 003 refines the existing P1 with traceability/resource-map evidence, so weighted refinement is 0.5x of the active P1 weight.
traceabilityChecks: Spec requirements and implementation/checklist evidence align on runtime mirror rename and /create:* command preservation.
resource-map Expected Read Paths, Expected Write Paths, and Verification Commands omit .codex/config.toml despite active Codex registry routing evidence.
Checklist consumer inventory names runtime agents, create commands, YAML assets, AGENTS.md, and sk-doc template references, but does not name the Codex registry consumer.
edgeCases: Findings registry appears stale because it reports zero open findings while JSONL/strategy carry active P1-001; direct evidence and strategy were used.
Resource-map omission was treated as a refinement of existing P1-001 rather than a separate P2 because it affects the same routing gate.
ruledOut: No new P0: no destructive, security, or auth-impacting traceability failure was found.
No separate P2 for resource-map omission: it materially supports P1-001 and should be fixed with the same Codex registry remediation.
Did not rerun exhausted runtime file presence checks from Iteration 001 or security boundary checks from Iteration 002.
